title: Booking Safety & Anti-Fraud
short_answer: Use only JVTO's official channels; JVTO never asks for full card numbers, CVV, banking passwords, or OTP codes.
detail_summary: Book and pay only through JVTO's official channels — the website, the official WhatsApp number, the official email, and JVTO's listed payment links and accounts. JVTO never asks for your full card number, CVV, online banking password, or one-time codes by chat or email. If an account number, link, or contact looks different, pause and verify through an official channel.

title: How to Book
short_answer: JVTO accepts bookings only through the official website; WhatsApp and email are for support, not for booking.
detail_summary: JVTO accepts bookings only through the official JVTO website. WhatsApp and email are for customer assistance only and do not create, confirm, modify, cancel, or transfer a booking.

title: Cancellation & Lifetime Package Credit
short_answer: A 48-hour cut-off: full cancellations 48h+ before Day 1 become Lifetime Package Credit; cash refunds apply only to partial passenger cancellations.
detail_summary: Full cancellation 48h+ before Day 1 converts the confirmed package to Lifetime Package Credit (never expires; same package, pax, and price; one transfer; not cash). Within 48h it is forfeited. Partial passenger cancellations get a cash refund of the cancelled travelers' price (100% at 48h+, 50% within 48h). Verified flight cancellations can reactivate the package for a 50% Recovery Fee.

title: Ijen Health Screening
short_answer: For Ijen crater hikes, JVTO coordinates a health screening with licensed medical staff when BBKSDA regulations require it.
detail_summary: When the East Java conservation authority (BBKSDA) requires it, crater access at Kawah Ijen depends on a short health screening. For affected tours JVTO coordinates this screening with licensed medical staff, and a verified certificate is needed at the crater gate.

title: Ijen Monthly Closure (Rijik Program)
short_answer: Kawah Ijen closes to all visitors on the first Friday of each month for the Rijik ecosystem-cleaning program; JVTO schedules tours around it so guests are unaffected.
detail_summary: Tourism Nature Park (TWA) Kawah Ijen closes completely to all tourism and mining activity on the **first Friday of every month** (00:00–23:59 WIB) for the **Rijik** ecosystem-cleaning program, which has run since March 2019.

title: What's Included
short_answer: JVTO tours are all-inclusive of transport, crew, entrance fees, water, and stated meals; flights, insurance, tips, and personal expenses are excluded.
detail_summary: Every JVTO package includes private transport, a dedicated driver and guide(s), all entrance fees and permits, drinking water, full pick-up to drop-off assistance, and meals as stated (at least breakfast). Bromo tours add the private 4WD jeep; Ijen tours add gas masks and trekking poles (and health-screening coordination where BBKSDA requires it); Bali-origin tours add the ferry crossing.

title: ISIC Student Pricing
short_answer: JVTO is an official ISIC provider offering student pricing on selected packages to verified ISIC cardholders.
detail_summary: JVTO is an official ISIC (International Student Identity Card) provider — provider ID 259268, listed publicly on the ISIC website. Verified ISIC cardholders can receive student pricing on selected packages.

title: Natural Phenomena Are Not Guaranteed
short_answer: JVTO cannot guarantee weather-dependent sights such as sunrise or Ijen's blue fire; itineraries are planned to maximise the chance.
detail_summary: Weather-dependent sights — a clear sunrise, or Ijen's blue fire — cannot be guaranteed, because cloud, weather, and volcanic gas are outside any operator's control. JVTO plans timing and routes to maximise the chance of good conditions, and may adjust timing or routing for safety.

title: Payment & Deposit
short_answer: A deposit secures the booking, with the balance due before the tour; exact amounts and deadlines are on the booking page.
detail_summary: A deposit (standard 20% of the booking value) secures a booking, with the balance due before the tour starts; close-to-departure bookings may require fuller payment. Exact amounts, accepted methods, and deadlines are shown at checkout and on the booking information page.

title: Police Escort for Large Groups
short_answer: For large groups, JVTO can coordinate an official traffic-police escort on some road segments, subject to authority approval.
detail_summary: For large groups (typically around 18 or more travelers), JVTO can coordinate an official traffic-police escort on certain road segments where the relevant Traffic Police unit approves it. This is a coordination service subject to authority approval, not a default inclusion. See the travel-guide page for how it works and any group incentives.

title: Private Tour Format
short_answer: This is a private tour with your own dedicated driver and guide(s); you are never combined with another group.

title: Crew & Language Standard
short_answer: English-speaking driver/guide standard; other languages subject to confirmation
detail_summary: private dedicated driver + guide (no shared groups)

title: Vehicle by Group Size
short_answer: We use an AC MPV for 1–3 guests and a Hiace for 4–9 guests.
limitation: Vehicle-by-pax is published; per-package luggage_rule is null in source (vehicle_luggage capability=partial). Oversized/special-item fit requires a live check, never a static promise.

title: Standard Rooming
short_answer: Standard rooming is arranged for the group; twin, double, separate rooms, or an extra room can be requested (subject to confirmation).
limitation: Generic rooming policy only. Exact room counts, child rooming, upgrades, and date-specific availability require a quote/live check.

title: All-Inclusive Baseline
short_answer: Every package includes private transport, a dedicated driver and guide(s), all entrance fees and permits, drinking water, meals as stated, and full pick-up to drop-off assistance.
included_baseline: meals as stated in the itinerary

short_answer: Private, dedicated vehicle for your group only.

short_answer: Dedicated private driver and guide(s); no shared group.

title: Private 4WD Jeep for Bromo
short_answer: Private 4WD jeep for the Bromo Sea of Sand and sunrise viewpoint.

title: Ijen Equipment (Gas Mask & Trekking Poles)

title: Ijen Health-Screening Coordination

title: East Java – Bali Ferry Crossing
short_answer: Ketapang–Gilimanuk ferry crossing for Bali-linked packages.

title: Standard Exclusions
short_answer: Excluded: international/domestic flights, visas, travel insurance, tips, personal expenses.

title: Kawah Ijen
short_answer: Active East Java volcano known for its turquoise acidic crater lake, pre-dawn "blue fire", and active sulfur mining, reached by a guided night hike.
typical_schedule: Hotel departure around midnight; the crater rim is reached near dawn for the blue fire and sunrise; finish mid-morning.
access_requirements: BBKSDA health screening and certificate when their regulations are in effect
gas mask and trekking poles (provided by JVTO)

title: Madakaripura Waterfall
short_answer: Java's tallest waterfall, in a narrow Probolinggo canyon near Bromo, reached by a short gorge walk where visitors get wet from the spray.
typical_schedule: Late-morning visit, often paired same-day with Bromo; a roughly 1.5 km gorge walk to the falls.
access_requirements: raincoat recommended (spray on the gorge walk)

title: Mount Bromo
short_answer: Active East Java volcano in the Tengger caldera "Sea of Sand", famous for its pre-dawn sunrise panorama reached by 4WD jeep.
typical_schedule: Pre-dawn departure (around 03:00) by 4WD jeep for the sunrise viewpoint, then a crossing of the sand sea and a short ascent to the crater rim; finish mid-morning.
access_requirements: TNBTS (Bromo Tengger Semeru National Park) ticket

title: Papuma Beach
short_answer: A white-sand beach with offshore rock formations and a headland viewpoint in Jember, East Java — the one coastal stop on JVTO's volcano circuits.
typical_schedule: Flexible daytime stop; a short headland walk for the viewpoints, used as a change of pace between volcano and waterfall days.
access_requirements: none (no permits or health screening required)

title: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall
short_answer: A wide multi-tiered "curtain" waterfall in Lumajang, East Java, often called "Java's Niagara", with an easy rim viewpoint and an optional steep canyon descent.
typical_schedule: Morning visit; an easy rim viewpoint, then an optional steep canyon descent; finish around midday.
access_requirements: optional steep canyon descent with ropes (the guide assesses fitness; the descent decision stays with the guest)

title: Previous tour dropoff as next pickup
Bromo Area to Bondowoso / Ijen Area
Bondowoso / Ijen Area to Ijen Crater
Ketapang Harbor to Gilimanuk / Bali side
Bali Hotel Area to Banyuwangi / Ijen Area

title: Why We Stay Near Ijen
short_answer: We stage here so guests can rest and prepare before the next activity. medical check can be arranged at hotel; dinner before Ijen preparation.
operational_notes: medical check can be arranged at hotel
dinner before Ijen preparation
midnight departure to Ijen

takeaway breakfast possible

short_answer: We stage here so guests can rest and prepare before the next activity. Artha Cottage / Lumajang area is the closest base; early morning start recommended 07:00–08:00 for waterfall access.
